subject: Social Sciences
title: Map Master
tagline: Read maps, identify compass directions and map symbols!

pins.name: North
facts: North is usually shown at the top of a map.
A compass needle points to magnetic North.

pins.name: South
facts: South is usually shown at the bottom of a map.
South is the opposite direction to North.

pins.name: East
facts: East is the direction the sun rises from.
East is usually shown on the right of a map.

pins.name: West
facts: West is the direction the sun sets in.
West is usually shown on the left of a map.

pins.name: Map Legend
facts: A legend (key) explains what map symbols mean.
Without a legend, map symbols would be hard to understand.

pins.name: Map Scale
facts: A scale shows how map distances relate to real distances.
A scale bar helps you measure real distance on a map.

pins.name: Grid Reference
facts: Grid references use letters and numbers to find a location.
Grid lines divide a map into squares for easy searching.

pins.name: Contour Line
facts: Contour lines join points of equal height on a map.
Contour lines close together show steep terrain.

questions.question: Which one is true: "North is usually shown at the top of a map."?
questions.feedbackFact: North is usually shown at the top of a map.

questions.question: Which one is true: "South is usually shown at the bottom of a map."?
questions.feedbackFact: South is usually shown at the bottom of a map.
